meaning: adj.出产的,易弯曲的,柔顺的,屈从的,易受影响的n.让步
examples: I mean, hypothetically a bond trading $0.60 on the dollar with an 8% coupon, a five-year bond that's yielding about 18%.
Borlaug began work at a Rockefeller Foundation-funded project in Mexico to increase wheat production by developing higher-yielding varieties of the crop.
He knew that the sources of the red-stained grains were generally closer to the North Pole than were the places yielding a high proportion of "clean" grains.
Pre-re munis maturing in 2014 were recently yielding 1.7%, the same yield offered by the Treasury.
Five years ago Zimmerman grew just one variety on a tenth of an acre, yielding 500 pounds.
